title: Ensure management console sign-in without MFA is monitored

input_row.title: Ensure management console sign-in without MFA is monitored
input_row.description: Real-time monitoring of API calls can be achieved by directing CloudTrail Logs to CloudWatch Logs or an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment, and establishing corresponding metric filters and alarms. It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for console logins that are not protected by multi-factor authentication (MFA).
input_row.rationale: CloudWatch is an AWS native service that allows you to observe and monitor resources and applications. CloudTrail logs can also be sent to an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment for monitoring and alerting. Monitoring for single-factor console logins will increase visibility into accounts that are not protected by MFA. These type of accounts are more susceptible to compromise and unauthorized access.
input_row.audit: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following to ensure that there is at least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail with the prescribed metric filters and alarms configured: 1. Identify the log group name that is configured for use with the active multi-region CloudTrail trail: • List all CloudTrail trails: aws cloudtrail describe-trails • Identify multi-region CloudTrail trails: Trails with "IsMultiRegionTrail" set to true • Note the value associated with "Name":<trail-name> • Note the <trail-log-group-name> within the value associated with "CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn" o Example: arn:aws:logs:<region>:<account-id>:log- group:<trail-log-group-name>:* • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail is active: Internal Only - General aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name> o ensure IsLogging is set to TRUE • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail captures all management events: aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name> o Ensure there is at least one event selector for a trail with IncludeManagementEvents set to true and ReadWriteType set to All 2. Get a list of all associated metric filters for the <trail-log-group-name> captured in step 1: aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> 3. Ensure the output from the above command contains the following: "filterPattern": "{ ($.eventName = "ConsoleLogin") && ($.additionalEventData.MFAUsed != "Yes") }" Or, to reduce false positives in case Single Sign-On (SSO) is used in the organization: "filterPattern": "{ ($.eventName = "ConsoleLogin") && ($.additionalEventData.MFAUsed != "Yes") && ($.userIdentity.type = "IAMUser") && ($.responseElements.ConsoleLogin = "Success") }" 4. Note the <no-mfa-console-signin-metric> value associated with the filterPattern from step 3. 5. Get a list of CloudWatch alarms, and filter on the <no-mfa-console-signin-metric> captured in step 4. aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query 'MetricAlarms[?MetricName== <no-mfa-console-signin-metric>]' 6. Note the AlarmActions value; this will provide the SNS topic ARN value. 7. Ensure there is at least one active subscriber to the SNS topic: aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> • At least one subscription should have "SubscriptionArn" with a valid AWS ARN. o Example of valid "SubscriptionArn": arn:aws:sns:<region>:<account- id>:<sns-topic-name>:<subscription-id> Internal Only - General
input_row.remediation: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following steps to set up the metric filter, alarm, SNS topic, and subscription: 1. Create a metric filter based on the provided filter pattern that checks for AWS Management Console sign-ins without MFA and uses the <trail-log-group-name> taken from audit step 1. aws logs put-metric-filter --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> --filter-name `<no-mfa-console-signin-metric>` --metric-transformations metricName= `<no-mfa-console-signin-metric>`,metricNamespace='CISBenchmark',metricValue=1 --filter-pattern '{ ($.eventName = "ConsoleLogin") && ($.additionalEventData.MFAUsed != "Yes") }' Or, to reduce false positives in case Single Sign-On (SSO) is used in the organization: aws logs put-metric-filter --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> --filter-name `<no-mfa-console-signin-metric>` --metric-transformations metricName= `<no-mfa-console-signin-metric>`,metricNamespace='CISBenchmark',metricValue=1 --filter-pattern '{ ($.eventName = "ConsoleLogin") && ($.additionalEventData.MFAUsed != "Yes") && ($.userIdentity.type = "IAMUser") && ($.responseElements.ConsoleLogin = "Success") }' Note: You can choose your own metricName and metricNamespace strings. Using the same metricNamespace for all Foundations Benchmark metrics will group them together. 2. Create an SNS topic that the alarm will notify: aws sns create-topic --name <sns-topic-name> Note: You can execute this command once and then reuse the same topic for all monitoring alarms. Note: Capture the TopicArn that is displayed when creating the SNS topic in step 2. 3. Create an SNS subscription for the topic created in step 2: aws sns subscribe --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> --protocol <sns-protocol> --notification-endpoint <sns-subscription-endpoints> Note: You can execute this command once and then reuse the same subscription for all monitoring alarms. Internal Only - General 4. Create an alarm that is associated with the CloudWatch Logs metric filter created in step 1 and the SNS topic created in step 2: aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name `<no-mfa-console-signin-alarm>` --metric-name `<no-mfa-console-signin-metric>` --statistic Sum --period 300 --threshold 1 --comparison-operator GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold --evaluation-periods 1 --namespace 'CISBenchmark' --alarm-actions <sns-topic-arn>
input_row.additional_information: Configuring a log metric filter and alarm on a multi-region (global) CloudTrail trail: • ensures that activities from all regions (both used and unused) are monitored • ensures that activities on all supported global services are monitored • ensures that all management events across all regions are monitored Filter pattern set to { ($.eventName = "ConsoleLogin") && ($.additionalEventData.MFAUsed != "Yes") && ($.userIdentity.type = "IAMUser") && ($.responseElements.ConsoleLogin = "Success"}: • reduces false alarms raised when a user logs in via SSO

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_cloudtrail_log_group_no_mfa_console_signin_monitoring

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
1. **Check for Active CloudTrail Trails**: 
   - Use the command: 
     ```bash
     aws cloudtrail describe-trails
     ```
   - Verify that at least one trail is multi-region (`IsMultiRegionTrail`: true) and currently logging (`IsLogging`: true).

2. **Identify CloudWatch Log Group**:
   - From the output of the previous command, extract the `CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn`.
   - The log group name is found within the ARN.

3. **Check CloudTrail Event Selectors**:
   - Command: 
     ```bash
     aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name>
     ```
   - Validate that there is an event selector with `IncludeManagementEvents` set to true and `ReadWriteType` set to All.

4. **Verify Metric Filters**:
   - Use the command:
     ```bash
     aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name>
     ```
   - Ensure the output contains a metric filter with the filter pattern:
     ```json
     "{ ($.eventName = \"ConsoleLogin\") && ($.additionalEventData.MFAUsed != \"Yes\") }"
     ```
   - Alternatively, for reducing false positives:
     ```json
     "{ ($.eventName = \"ConsoleLogin\") && ($.additionalEventData.MFAUsed != \"Yes\") && ($.userIdentity.type = \"IAMUser\") && ($.responseElements.ConsoleLogin = \"Success\") }"
     ```

5. **Check CloudWatch Alarms**:
   - Command:
     ```bash
     aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query 'MetricAlarms[?MetricName==<no-mfa-console-signin-metric>]'
     ```
   - Inspect the `AlarmActions` to get the SNS topic ARN.

6. **Validate SNS Subscriptions**:
   - Use the command:
     ```bash
     aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn>
     ```
   - Ensure that there is at least one valid `SubscriptionArn`.

**Pass/Fail Criteria**:
- The audit passes if:
  - There is at least one active multi-region CloudTrail.
  - CloudWatch log group is configured for the CloudTrail.
  - The CloudTrail is capturing management events.
  - Metric filters for MFA-less sign-ins are present.
  - At least one alarm is present on the metric filter.
  - The SNS topic associated with the alarms has at least one active subscriber. 
- If any of these conditions are not met, the audit fails.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The tasks outlined can be effectively executed using AWS APIs, boto3, or AWS CLI, which aligns with the capabilities of these tools for automating security checks.
- AWS SDK capabilities, AWS CLI commands, and CloudFormation can all be leveraged to automate the checks described in the assessment.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8

- The automation details are mostly technically correct. The AWS CLI commands provided are valid for checking CloudTrail configurations, CloudWatch metric filters, and SNS subscriptions.
- The program name `aws_cloudtrail_log_group_no_mfa_console_signin_monitoring` is descriptive but could be more concise. It does follow the convention of specifying the service and the specific security check.
- The metric filter pattern is correctly specified, and the alternative pattern for reducing false positives is a good addition.
- There is a minor omission in the CloudWatch Alarms check: the command should include a specific metric name or pattern to ensure it accurately targets the intended alarm.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- The program name could be simplified to `aws_cloudtrail_mfa_console_signin_monitoring` for clarity and conciseness.
- The check for CloudWatch Alarms should specify the metric name more explicitly to avoid ambiguity. The command could be refined to:
  ```bash
  aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query 'MetricAlarms[?MetricName==`<no-mfa-console-signin-metric>`]'
  ```
- Consider using AWS Config rules to continuously monitor these configurations, which could enhance the automation and compliance monitoring process.
- Ensure that the CloudTrail is configured to log to the correct CloudWatch Log Group by verifying the ARN matches the intended log group.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The assessment is well-structured and aligns with AWS best practices for security monitoring. The use of AWS CLI commands is appropriate, and the logic for checking each component is sound.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** Modify

- While the overall assessment is solid, I recommend modifying the program name for clarity and refining the CloudWatch Alarms check to ensure specificity in targeting the correct metric.
- Additionally, consider integrating AWS Config for ongoing compliance checks, which could provide a more robust solution.
